vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF for tightening story implementation start and completion gates

plan.title: fix(skills): add story start gate and lifecycle checkpoint contract

narratives.Problem: Story implementation can currently begin without a universal dirty-work prompt, canonical promote/activate bridge in the build skill, story-sized branch/checkpoint guidance, or a build-skill completion rule.
narratives.Action: Add hard implementation-unit gate language to generated AGENTS.md, the build skill, and the swarm skill, with content tests pinning the new contract.

items.title: AGENTS template contains dirty-work story start gate

narrative.Acceptance: templates/agents-entry.md and regenerated AGENTS.md require git status --short --branch before starting or switching implementation stories, and require the operator prompt when unrelated dirty work is present.

items.title: Build skill bridges lifecycle before preflight

narrative.Acceptance: skills/deft-directive-build/SKILL.md resolves one target story by default, runs task scope:promote / task scope:activate as needed, and runs task vbrief:preflight before code writing.

items.title: Story scope is default branch and checkpoint unit

narrative.Acceptance: The build skill defaults to one story per branch/PR and requires a checkpoint commit after each completed story before starting another story unless the operator explicitly approves batching.
The build skill requires task scope:complete -- <active-story> after checks pass and before final handoff.
The swarm skill states that multi-story batching is allowed only after explicit operator approval or an approved allocation plan with rationale.
tests/content asserts the dirty-work prompt, scope activation, preflight, one-story default, checkpoint commit, scope completion, and swarm batching approval language.
